metadata.description: A discrete platform capability — the unit of architectural capability tracked, normalized, and gap-analysed by DAV. A Knowledge-family entity: its lifecycle is curation (proposed -> under-review -> canonical -> deprecated), not provisioning, and it is never realized by a provider. The family tag disambiguates the collision-prone bare word 'Capability' from any Resource type.

name.description: Canonical capability name (single normalized handle). The curation lifecycle converges aliases onto this.

description.description: What the capability is and the architectural concern it addresses.

pillar.description: Top-level grouping the capability rolls up to (e.g. a maturity-framework pillar).

domain.description: Capability domain (e.g. one of the DCM capability domains) this capability belongs to.

curation_state.description: Position in the curation lifecycle — the Knowledge-family interpretation of the four states (provenance-first; promotion to canonical is a curation act, applied by Policy, not by a provider).

normalized_handle.description: The canonical handle a Capability resolves to once curated to `canonical`. Other Knowledge entities (aliases, use cases) bind to this via typed references rather than free-text capability names.

relationships.description: normalized_to — this capability is mapped to one or more canonical taxonomy terms (e.g. the DCM capability taxonomy). A declarative typed reference; the mapping act is Policy.
This capability depends on another capability (capability-map edge). Forms an acyclic capability DAG; traversal/ordering is Policy.
